Where We Sleep

Our Châteaux

Week One · May 9–16

Aile Périgord

Vieux Mareuil, Dordogne, France
400
sqm
6
Bedrooms
4
Bathrooms
12
Guests Max

Set within the grounds of a former Templar manor, this elegantly restored wing marries timeless history with modern comfort. The wing is entirely reserved for guests — your own private estate in the heart of the Périgord, surrounded by rolling countryside, ancient stone walls, and a beautiful heated pool.

  • Heated Pool
  • Tennis Court
  • Pétanque
  • Table Tennis
  • Vegetable Garden
  • French Garden
  • Terrace
  • Full Kitchen
  • Panoramic Views
  • Wi-Fi

Included Services

  • Daily breakfast delivered each morning
  • Mid-stay housekeeping
  • 24/7 concierge service
  • Private chef on request
  • Transfers on request

Available on Request

  • Airport transfers
  • Private chef service
  • Grocery delivery
  • In-house spa treatments
  • Bespoke activities & babysitting
Week Two · May 16–23

Château Troplong Mondot

Saint-Émilion, Bordeaux, France
500
sqm
5
Bedrooms
5
Bathrooms
10
Guests Max

Perched at the highest point in Saint-Émilion, this 18th-century Premier Grand Cru Classé estate offers an unmatched immersive wine experience. Wake to 360° views over the vines and the UNESCO village. A butler, daily breakfast from Michelin-starred chef David Charrier, and a private vineyard tour and tasting are all included.

  • Premier Grand Cru Classé
  • Heated Pool
  • Butler Included
  • Vineyard Tour + Tasting
  • Daily Breakfast
  • Michelin Restaurant
  • 360° Views
  • 2 Living Rooms
  • Park & Gardens

Included Services

  • Dedicated butler throughout stay
  • Private vineyard tour & wine tasting
  • Daily breakfast by Chef David Charrier
  • Daily housekeeping

Available Extras (not included)

  • Lunch or dinner at the Château
  • Les Belles Perdrix Michelin-star restaurant
  • Additional rooms via Les Clefs
  • Activities inside or outside the estate
  • City tax

Getting There & Around

Travel Logistics

Journey Outline

Fly

Fly into Bordeaux–Mérignac (BOD)

The most convenient hub. Direct transatlantic flights available. Alternative: Paris CDG then TGV or connecting flight.

Drive

Bordeaux → Vieux Mareuil (May 9)

~2h 15min northeast toward Périgord Vert. Rental car essential. The drive through Bergerac wine country is lovely — consider a lunch stop en route.

Drive

Vieux Mareuil → Saint-Émilion (May 16)

~2h drive southwest back toward Bordeaux. Beautiful route through the Bergerac wine region — stop at Château de Monbazillac for a tasting.

Depart

Fly home from Bordeaux (May 23)

Saint-Émilion to Bordeaux Airport is just 45 minutes. Early check-out leaves time for a final stroll through the medieval village.

Practical Tips

🚗 Rental Car

Mid-size SUV or minivan recommended. Book in advance for May. GPS essential — rural Dordogne roads can be tricky. Both châteaux have private parking.

✈️ Airports

Bordeaux (BOD) — 2h15 to Périgord, 45min to Saint-Émilion
Bergerac (EGC) — 1h15 to Périgord (UK flights mainly)
Paris CDG — TGV to Bordeaux ~2h, then drive

📱 Getting Around

Public transport is sparse in rural Dordogne — driving is essential. Saint-Émilion is very walkable; leave the car at Troplong Mondot and stroll down to the village.

💶 Money

Cards widely accepted. Carry cash for Sarlat market (Wed & Sat). ATMs in Sarlat, Bergerac, and Saint-Émilion town.

Where to Eat

Memorable Meals

Périgord · Week 1
★★ Michelin

Le Grand Bleu

Sarlat-la-Canéda

Chef Marc Benoît's acclaimed table in medieval Sarlat. Creative Périgordine cuisine — foie gras, duck, and black truffle elevated to a fine art.

Périgord · Week 1
Relais & Châteaux

Le Moulin de l'Abbaye

Brantôme — "Venice of Périgord"

A riverside Relais & Châteaux restaurant beside the Dronne. The terrace in May is magical — perfect for a long family Sunday lunch.

Périgord · Week 1
Market & Feast

Sarlat Saturday Market

Place de la Liberté, Sarlat

The legendary market fills medieval Sarlat every Wednesday and Saturday. Load up on foie gras, Périgord truffles, duck rillettes, and Monbazillac wine for a château dinner.

Périgord · Week 1
★ Michelin

La Table du Terroir

Brantôme area

Seasonal local cooking at its finest — wild mushrooms, river fish, and walnut-based desserts. The soul of Périgord on a plate.

Saint-Émilion · Week 2
★ Michelin · On-Site

Les Belles Perdrix

Château Troplong Mondot — your home

Your own Michelin-starred restaurant, steps from your bedroom. Chef David Charrier's seasonal French cuisine on a hilltop terrace overlooking the vines. Book a special birthday dinner here.

Saint-Émilion · Week 2
★★ Michelin

La Table de Pavie

Hôtel de Pavie, Saint-Émilion

Chef Yannick Alléno's two-star temple to Bordeaux terroir. A journey from vine to plate — langoustine, Bazas beef, and an extraordinary cellar of Grands Crus.

Saint-Émilion · Week 2
★ Michelin

Logis de la Cadène

Place du Marché, Saint-Émilion

Founded 1848, now owned by Château Angelus — the oldest restaurant in Saint-Émilion. Garden terrace, classic French gastronomy, exceptional wine list.

Saint-Émilion · Week 2
Wine Bar · Local Favourite

L'Envers du Décor

Rue du Clocher, Saint-Émilion

Where the winemakers drink. 500 wine references, sunny terrace, superb charcuterie and cheese boards. Perfect for a relaxed evening.

Forecast & Climate

May Weather

May is one of the finest months to visit Southwest France — warm, lush, and before the summer crowds. Expect mild sunny days with occasional afternoon showers.

🏰 Périgord (May 9–16)

Average High66–72°F · 19–22°C
Average Low50–55°F · 10–13°C
Sunshine~7 hrs/day ☀️
Rain chance~25% · bring a light jacket
Pool weatherSome days — evenings are cool

🍇 Saint-Émilion (May 16–23)

Average High68–74°F · 20–23°C
Average Low52–57°F · 11–14°C
Sunshine~7.5 hrs/day ☀️
Rain chance~20% · generally pleasant
VinesFully leafed & gorgeous in May 🌿
🌸 Packing Tips: Light layers are key — warm afternoons, cool evenings. A packable rain jacket. Walking shoes for cobblestones. Smart casual for Michelin dinners (no jeans/sneakers). Sunscreen for terrace dining.

Things to Do

Explore the Region

Périgord · Week One

May 9–16 · Dordogne
🏰

Château de Beynac & Castelnaud

Two medieval fortresses facing each other across the Dordogne — former front lines of the Hundred Years' War. Beynac is the most dramatic; Castelnaud has a world-class medieval warfare museum.

🎨

Lascaux IV Cave Paintings

A full-scale replica of the world's most celebrated prehistoric cave art — 17,000-year-old masterpieces in stunning detail. One of the most moving cultural experiences in France.

🌿

Jardins de Marqueyssac

150,000 hand-sculpted boxwood hedges cascading above the Dordogne, with a belvedere 130m over the river. In bloom and gorgeous in May.

🚣

Canoe on the Dordogne River

Paddle past La Roque-Gageac, under the walls of Beynac, and through the valley of castles. Half or full-day rentals from La Roque-Gageac.

🏘️

Sarlat-la-Canéda Old Town

The finest preserved medieval town in France. Amber-stone streets, twice-weekly market, and excellent foie gras shops inside 15th-century buildings.

🎈

Hot Air Balloon at Sunrise

Drift over castles, forests and the river at dawn. Several operators run flights near Sarlat — a spectacular way to mark the celebration.

Saint-Émilion · Week Two

May 16–23 · Bordeaux
🍷

Troplong Mondot Vineyard Tour

Included in your stay: a private 4x4 tour of the estate and tasting of their Premier Grand Cru Classé — one of the great wines of Saint-Émilion.

⛪

Saint-Émilion Medieval Village

UNESCO World Heritage Site. Walk cobbled lanes to the rock-carved monolithic church, underground catacombs, and the 12th-century tower for panoramic views.

🚲

E-Bike through the Vineyards

Electric bikes available at Troplong Mondot. Pedal past Château Pavie, Château Figeac, and rolling Grand Cru vineyards — 20 minutes to the village.

🏙️

Day Trip to Bordeaux City

45 minutes away. La Cité du Vin wine museum, the Miroir d'Eau water mirror, Place de la Bourse, and excellent shopping along Rue Sainte-Catherine.

🦞

Arcachon Bay & Dune du Pilat

1 hour away — Europe's tallest sand dune and the Arcachon Bay with fresh oysters straight from the water. A wonderful day trip.

🏛️

Pomerol & Médoc Wine Route

Visit legendary estates — Pétrus, Cheval Blanc, and the grand Médoc châteaux. Private tastings can be arranged through the Troplong Mondot concierge.

The Reason We're Here

To Rich — 65 & Retired

Two weeks. Two châteaux. The golden countryside of the Dordogne, then the sun-drenched vines of Saint-Émilion. This trip is a toast to everything you've built, everything you've given us, and everything that still lies ahead. May these weeks be full of long lunches, cold rosé, warm evenings on the terrace, and the kind of laughter that only happens when family gathers somewhere truly extraordinary.

Rich & Teresa Katie & Phil Kevin, Lindsay & Brooks Nicole & Ollie Sarah & Nick
Santé, Rich. ✦
